days.title: Arrival in Paris
days.subtitle: Gateway to European art and culture

timeline.title: Arrive at Charles de Gaulle Airport
timeline.detail: Land at Paris CDG Airport. Collect luggage and proceed to customs/immigration.
timeline.mapQuery: Charles de Gaulle Airport, Paris, France

timeline.title: RER B Train to Paris City Center
timeline.detail: Take the RER B train from CDG Airport directly to Gare de l'Est station in central Paris (approximately 35 minutes).

timeline.title: Hotel Check-in
timeline.detail: Arrive at Hotel Gare de l'Est and check in. Drop off luggage and freshen up.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gare de l'Est, Paris, France

timeline.title: Lunch in Le Marais District
timeline.detail: Enjoy authentic French cuisine at a traditional bistro in the historic Le Marais neighborhood, known for art galleries and architecture.

timeline.title: Explore Sainte-Chapelle
timeline.detail: Visit this stunning Gothic chapel famous for its 13th-century stained glass windows and intricate architecture. Perfect for art and history enthusiasts.

timeline.title: Seine River Walk
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely evening stroll along the Seine riverbanks, enjoying views of historic bridges, riverside architecture, and the Parisian atmosphere.

timeline.title: Aperitif at Café de Flore
timeline.detail: Stop at this iconic Parisian café for wine and light appetizers while watching the sunset over the city.

timeline.title: Dinner in Latin Quarter
timeline.detail: Dine at a traditional French restaurant in the charming Latin Quarter, surrounded by medieval streets and academic architecture.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel
timeline.detail: Head back to Hotel Gare de l'Est via metro or taxi. Rest and prepare for Day 2 exploration.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gare de l'Est, Paris, France

highlights: Arrive at Charles de Gaulle Airport
Check-in to hotel near Gare de l'Est
localTips: Buy Paris Museum Pass (2-day) for unlimited access
Get RER B train ticket from airport (€11.45)
Hotel near Gare de l'Est for easy onward travel

days.title: Louvre & Latin Quarter
days.subtitle: Art masterpieces and historic streets

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Start your day with breakfast at Hotel Gare de l'Est before heading to the Louvre. Fuel up for a day of art and exploration.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gare de l'Est, Paris, France

timeline.title: Metro to Louvre
timeline.detail: Take Metro Line 4 or 5 from Gare de l'Est station to Palais-Royal-Musée du Louvre. Journey takes approximately 15-20 minutes.
timeline.mapQuery: Palais-Royal-Musée du Louvre Metro Station, Paris, France

timeline.title: Louvre Museum Entry
timeline.detail: Arrive at the iconic Louvre Museum. Start with the glass pyramid entrance and plan to spend 3-4 hours exploring masterpieces including the Mona Lisa and Venus de Milo.

timeline.title: Lunch at Café Mollien
timeline.detail: Take a lunch break at Café Mollien located within the Louvre. Enjoy French cuisine with a view overlooking the Tuileries Garden.
timeline.mapQuery: Café Mollien, Louvre Museum, Paris, France

timeline.title: Tuileries Garden Walk
timeline.detail: Exit the Louvre and stroll through the beautiful Tuileries Garden (Jardin des Tuileries). Enjoy the historic gardens, fountains, and sculptures while walking towards the Place de la Concorde.

timeline.title: Latin Quarter Exploration
timeline.detail: Walk or take Metro to the Latin Quarter (Quartier Latin). Explore charming medieval streets, browse independent bookshops, and visit historic cafés frequented by intellectuals and artists.

timeline.title: Notre-Dame & Île de la Cité
timeline.detail: Walk to Île de la Cité to view Notre-Dame Cathedral's exterior and stunning Gothic architecture. Stroll around the island, cross the historic bridges, and enjoy the Seine River views.
timeline.mapQuery: Notre-Dame de Chartres, Île de la Cité, Paris, France

timeline.title: Dinner in Latin Quarter
timeline.detail: Enjoy an authentic French dinner at a traditional bistro in the Latin Quarter. Try local specialties like coq au vin or duck confit in this vibrant neighborhood.

timeline.title: Evening Seine Walk
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely walk along the Seine River banks. Experience Paris's magical evening atmosphere with illuminated bridges and riverside views.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel
timeline.detail: Take Metro Line 4 back from Saint-Michel-Notre-Dame to Gare de l'Est. Arrive at hotel and rest after a full day of art and culture exploration.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gare de l'Est, Paris, France

highlights: Louvre Museum - Mona Lisa, Venus de Milo
localTips: Book Louvre tickets online to skip queues
Visit early morning for fewer crowds
Café coffee culture in Latin Quarter - try café crème
Use Museum Pass for free entry

days.title: Impressionism & Montmartre
days.subtitle: Artistic heritage and bohemian charm

timeline.title: Hotel breakfast & checkout
timeline.detail: Enjoy breakfast at Hotel Gare de l'Est before heading out for the day's artistic adventures in Montmartre and the Musée d'Orsay.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gare de l'Est, Paris, France

timeline.title: Metro to Musée d'Orsay
timeline.detail: Take Metro Line 4 or 8 from Gare de l'Est towards the museum. Travel time approximately 15-20 minutes.

timeline.title: Musée d'Orsay - Impressionist masterpieces
timeline.detail: Explore the world's finest collection of Impressionist and Post-Impressionist art, including works by Monet, Renoir, and Van Gogh housed in the former railway station.
timeline.mapQuery: Musée d'Orsay, 1 Rue de la Légion d'Honneur, Paris, France

timeline.title: Lunch at museum café or nearby bistro
timeline.detail: Take a break and enjoy French cuisine at the Musée d'Orsay café or walk to a local bistro in the 7th arrondissement for authentic Parisian lunch.
timeline.mapQuery: Café Campana, Musée d'Orsay, Paris, France

timeline.title: Metro to Montmartre district
timeline.detail: Travel via Metro Line 12 to Abbesses station, the heart of Montmartre. Journey takes approximately 25 minutes from the museum.

timeline.title: Montmartre hilltop wandering
timeline.detail: Stroll through charming cobblestone streets, narrow alleyways, and bohemian neighborhoods discovering artist studios, vintage shops, and panoramic views across Paris.

timeline.title: Sacré-Cœur Basilica visit
timeline.detail: Climb the steps to this iconic white basilica perched at the top of Montmartre for stunning architecture and breathtaking city views from the dome.

timeline.title: Place du Tertre artist square
timeline.detail: Visit the historic artist square where painters and caricaturists display their work, capturing the bohemian spirit that inspired centuries of creative talent.

timeline.title: Apéritif at local wine bar
timeline.detail: Enjoy a pre-dinner drink at a charming Montmartre wine bar with local wines and cheese to experience the neighborhood's evening atmosphere.

timeline.title: Local bistro dinner experience
timeline.detail: Dine at a traditional Parisian bistro in Montmartre, savoring classic French cuisine like coq au vin or duck confit with local wine in an intimate setting.

timeline.title: Metro return to hotel
timeline.detail: Take the Metro back to Hotel Gare de l'Est from Abbesses station to rest after a full day exploring Paris's artistic heritage.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gare de l'Est, Paris, France

highlights: Artist square at Place du Tertre
localTips: Musée d'Orsay best visited afternoon
Montmartre: avoid tourist trap restaurants near Sacré-Cœur
Take funicular up to basilica or climb stairs
Dinner in Montmartre backstreets for authentic Paris

days.title: Train to Amsterdam
days.subtitle: Journey through European countryside

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Continental breakfast at Hotel Gare de l'Est before checkout. Fuel up for the morning palace visit and train journey ahead.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gare de l'Est, Paris, France

timeline.title: Hotel Checkout
timeline.detail: Check out of Hotel Gare de l'Est. Store luggage securely or arrange for it to be held until train departure.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gare de l'Est, Paris, France

timeline.title: Train to Versailles or Compiègne
timeline.detail: Take SNCF train from Gare de l'Est to either Versailles-Chantiers (45min) or Compiègne (50min). Enjoy the French countryside en route.

timeline.title: Palace of Versailles or Compiègne
timeline.detail: Explore the opulent Palace of Versailles with its Hall of Mirrors and stunning gardens, or visit the elegant Palace of Compiègne with its rich imperial history. Tour takes 2-3 hours depending on pace.

timeline.title: Lunch at Palace Café
timeline.detail: Grab lunch at a café near the palace. Light French cuisine or café fare to refuel before returning to Paris.

timeline.title: Return Train to Paris
timeline.detail: Take SNCF train back to Gare de l'Est (approximately 45-50 minutes). Retrieve luggage and prepare for Thalys departure.

timeline.title: Arrive Gare de l'Est, Luggage Check
timeline.detail: Arrive at Gare de l'Est. Collect luggage and check in for the Thalys train. Allow time for security and boarding procedures.

timeline.title: Thalys High-Speed Train Departure
timeline.detail: Depart Paris on Thalys high-speed train to Amsterdam. Enjoy first-class comfort and European countryside views during the 3 hour 15 minute journey.
timeline.mapQuery: Gare de l'Est, Paris, France to Amsterdam Centraal Station, Amsterdam, Netherlands

timeline.title: Arrive Amsterdam Centraal Station
timeline.detail: Arrive at Amsterdam Centraal Station. Take local tram or walk to your hotel near the station. Check in and settle into your room.

timeline.title: Evening Canal-Side Dinner
timeline.detail: Enjoy dinner at a traditional Dutch restaurant along one of Amsterdam's picturesque canals. Try local specialties like bitterballen, stroopwafels, or fresh fish while watching the canal-side lights.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel & Rest
timeline.detail: Walk back to your hotel near Centraal Station. Rest and prepare for Day 5 exploring Amsterdam's museums and cycling culture.
timeline.mapQuery: Amsterdam Centraal Station area, Amsterdam, Netherlands

highlights: Morning visit to Versailles or Palace of Compiegne
Arrive Amsterdam, check-in near Centraal Station
localTips: Book Thalys train in advance for better rates
Depart Paris 14:00, arrive Amsterdam 17:15
Hotel within 5-min walk of Amsterdam Centraal
Get Amsterdam GVB transit card at station

days.title: Amsterdam Canals & Museums
days.subtitle: Golden Age architecture and Dutch Masters

timeline.title: Breakfast at hotel
timeline.detail: Continental breakfast at Hotel Amsterdam Centraal with coffee and pastries to fuel your day.

timeline.title: Canal ring cycling tour
timeline.detail: Rent bikes and explore Amsterdam's UNESCO-listed canal ring, cycling past 17th-century Golden Age architecture and picturesque bridges.

timeline.title: Lunch at waterfront café
timeline.detail: Stop for traditional Dutch lunch with pancakes, herring, or cheese at a casual waterfront café along the canals.

timeline.title: Anne Frank House
timeline.detail: Visit this poignant museum in the hidden annex where Anne Frank wrote her famous diary during WWII. Book tickets in advance.
timeline.mapQuery: Anne Frank House, Prinsengracht 263-267, Amsterdam, Netherlands

timeline.title: Rijksmuseum Dutch Golden Age
timeline.detail: Explore masterpieces by Vermeer, Rembrandt, and Hals, featuring iconic works like 'Girl with a Pearl Earring' and 'The Night Watch'.

timeline.title: Amsterdam Museum
timeline.detail: Discover Amsterdam's history from medieval times to present day through interactive exhibitions and galleries spanning multiple floors.

timeline.title: Dinner at traditional brown café
timeline.detail: Experience authentic Dutch atmosphere in a cozy brown café (bruine kroeg) with dark wood interiors, sampling local beers and comfort food.
timeline.mapQuery: Café de Dokter, Lauriergracht 86, Amsterdam, Netherlands

timeline.title: Evening canal walk
timeline.detail: Stroll along the illuminated canals in the evening, enjoying the reflections of historic buildings and street lamps in the water.

timeline.title: Return to hotel
timeline.detail: Head back to Hotel Amsterdam Centraal for rest after a full day of art, architecture, and Dutch culture.

localTips: Book Anne Frank House tickets online weeks ahead
Rent bike for authentic Amsterdam experience
Watch for trams while cycling
Brown cafés open early for coffee culture
Try stroopwafels and bitterballen

days.title: Van Gogh & Architecture
days.subtitle: Modern art and Dutch design heritage

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Start your day with breakfast at Hotel Amsterdam Centraal. Enjoy Dutch pastries and coffee before heading out to explore the museums.

timeline.title: Van Gogh Museum
timeline.detail: Explore the world's largest collection of Vincent van Gogh's works, including Sunflowers and Starry Night. Plan for 2-3 hours to fully appreciate the masterpieces.
timeline.mapQuery: Van Gogh Museum, Museumplein, Amsterdam, Netherlands

timeline.title: Lunch in Jordaan District
timeline.detail: Enjoy a light lunch at a local café in the charming Jordaan neighborhood near the museums. Try traditional Dutch cheese or a fresh sandwich.

timeline.title: Stedelijk Museum
timeline.detail: Visit this contemporary and modern art museum featuring works by Picasso, Matisse, and Mondrian. Spend 1.5-2 hours enjoying the rotating exhibitions.

timeline.title: Canal Architecture Photography Walk
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely walk along Amsterdam's picturesque canals, photographing the historic architecture, charming bridges, and houseboats. Rent bikes if preferred.

timeline.title: Jewish Quarter Historical Walk
timeline.detail: Explore the historic Jewish Quarter (Jodenbuurt), visiting the Portuguese Synagogue and learning about Amsterdam's Jewish heritage. Visit the Anne Frank House if time permits.

timeline.title: Albert Cuyp Market Visit
timeline.detail: Browse this famous local market in De Pijp neighborhood with food stalls, fresh produce, and local specialties. Perfect for grabbing dinner ingredients or street food.

timeline.title: Dinner at Local Restaurant
timeline.detail: Enjoy a traditional Dutch dinner featuring local dishes like bitterballen, herring, or stamppot at a cozy neighborhood restaurant near your hotel.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel & Rest
timeline.detail: Take a bike or tram back to Hotel Amsterdam Centraal. Relax and prepare for the next day of your Amsterdam adventure.

highlights: Van Gogh Museum - world's largest collection
localTips: Van Gogh Museum book online in advance
Visit Stedelijk Museum for Dutch design
Albert Cuyp Market for food & local goods
Photographer's paradise along Herengracht
Canal-side dinner overlooking water

days.title: Amsterdam Free Time & Departure
days.subtitle: Last-minute exploration before heading home

timeline.title: Hotel Check-out
timeline.detail: Complete checkout from Hotel Amsterdam Centraal and store luggage at reception for later pickup before airport transfer.

timeline.title: Breakfast at Local Café
timeline.detail: Enjoy a traditional Dutch breakfast with fresh pastries, cheese, and coffee at a cozy neighborhood café near the hotel.

timeline.title: Bloemenmarkt - Floating Flower Market
timeline.detail: Explore Amsterdam's iconic floating flower market with vibrant tulips, plants, and local souvenirs along the canal. Perfect for last-minute gifts and photography.

timeline.title: Shopping & Street Time
timeline.detail: Stroll through Kalverstraat and nearby shopping streets for last-minute Dutch souvenirs, books, and local products.

timeline.title: Lunch at Canal-side Restaurant
timeline.detail: Savor a leisurely lunch with traditional Dutch cuisine and canal views before afternoon activities.

timeline.title: Cycling to Vondelpark
timeline.detail: Rent bicycles and cycle through Amsterdam's charming streets to the beautiful Vondelpark for a final peaceful moment in the city.

timeline.title: Free Time at Vondelpark
timeline.detail: Relax in the park, visit the nearby Van Gogh Museum, or simply enjoy the Dutch landscape and street performers.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel & Luggage Pickup
timeline.detail: Cycle back to Hotel Amsterdam Centraal, collect luggage from reception, and prepare for airport departure.

timeline.title: Airport Transfer
timeline.detail: Depart for Amsterdam Airport Schiphol via taxi, train, or arranged shuttle service with plenty of time for international flight.

highlights: Morning visit to Bloemenmarkt (Floating Flower Market)
Free time for shopping or last museum visit
localTips: Flight timing dependent on booking
Airport train (Schiphol Express) 25 min from Centraal Station
Tax refund at airport for EU VAT
Allow 3 hours before international flight

hotels.name: Hotel Gare de l'Est
hotels.note: 3-min walk from Gare de l'Est Station

budget.note: Per person, economy class with Qatar Airways or similar (avoid Gulf hubs - routing via Istanbul or others)
Train Schiphol to Centraal Station, €5.50 per person
€39 per person, includes Louvre, Musée d'Orsay, Versailles
€45 per person, includes Van Gogh, Rijksmuseum, 30+ museums
€35/day per person (similar pricing, Dutch food slightly cheaper)
3-day tourist card €27 per person, some walking covered
Budget for unplanned items, café extras

urgent.label: Book Anne Frank House tickets
urgent.note: Sells out weeks in advance, book immediately

urgent.label: Book Thalys train Paris-Amsterdam
urgent.note: Reserve high-speed train 2-4 weeks ahead for best rates

urgent.label: Purchase Paris Museum Pass
urgent.note: 2-day pass offers best value, buy online before arrival

tickets.name: Thalys: Paris Gare de l'Est → Amsterdam Centraal
